zh.Combe Martin, Devon, England: 英國戴文可姆 馬丁
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Frederick Orchard於1432年出生在英國戴文可姆 馬丁時，他的父親Richard Orchard20歲，母親Elizabeth Glader27歲。他於1451年與Agnes Crouche在英國英格蘭戴文哈福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1480年8月30日在英國戴文哈福德享年48歲。

zh-hans.Combe Martin, Devon, England: 英国戴文可姆 馬丁
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Frederick Orchard于1432年出生在英国戴文可姆 馬丁时，他的父亲Richard Orchard20岁，母亲Elizabeth Glader27岁。他于1451年与Agnes Crouche在英国英格兰戴文哈福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1480年8月30日在英国戴文哈福德享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Orchard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: topographic or occupational name from residence near (or perhaps employment at) an orchard, Middle English orchard (Old English ortgeard, orceard).

habitational name from any of the places called Orchard. Those in Devon and Somerset are named from Old English ortgeard, orceard (a compound of wort, wyrt ‘plant’ (later associated with Latin hortus ‘garden’) + geard ‘yard, enclosure’), while East and West Orchard near Shaftesbury in Dorset derive from Primitive Welsh ar ‘beside, in front of, facing’ + ced ‘wood’.
rare variant of Scottish   Urquhart  . Compare   Orahood  .

variants.name: Urquhart
meanings: Scottish (Ross and Cromarty): habitational name from any of various places in Scotland so called, notably the barony of Urquhart (Inverness), or places in Ross and Cromarty, Morayshire, and Fife. All are thought to derive from Pictish ar ‘on, by’ + Pictish carden ‘enclosure’ or ‘wood, copse’. Some bearers of this name trace their descent from William de Urchard, who fought for Robert the Bruce between 1297 and 1328.

variants.name: Orahood
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Orchard   3, itself a rare English variant of Scottish   Urquhart  .

History: The Orahoods can trace their origin to Alexander, Joshua, and Thomas Orahood, who in the second half of the 18th century lived in VA and are believed to come to America from Scotland. Their surname is also listed in the forms Orrahud, Arahood, Arrowhood, and Orchard, the last one being the most likely original form.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Frederick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredrick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredric
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Frederick Orchard was born in 1432, in Combe Martin, Devon, England, his father, Richard Orchard, was 20 and his mother, Elizabeth Glader, was 27. He married Agnes Crouche in 1451, in Chagford, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 30 August 1480, in Chagford, Devon, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Frederick Orchard est né en 1432, à Combe Martin, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Richard Orchard, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Glader, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Crouche en 1451, à Chagford, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 30 août 1480, à Chagford, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Frederick Orchard 1432 in Combe Martin, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Orchard 20 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Glader 27. Er hat 1451 in Chagford, Devon, England, United Kingdom Agnes Crouche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 30. August 1480 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Chagford, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Frederick Orchard è nato il nel 1432 nel Combe Martin, Devon, England, il suo padre, Richard Orchard, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Glader, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Crouche nel 1451, nel Chagford, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 30 agosto 1480, nel Chagford, Devon, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Frederick Orchardが1432年にイギリスデボンコンベ マルティンで生まれた時，父Richard Orchardは20歳，母Elizabeth Gladerは27歳であった。1451年にイギリスイギリスデボンチャグフォードでAgnes Croucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1480年8月30日にイギリスデボンチャグフォードで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Frederick Orchard(이)가 1432년 영국 데폰 코브 마틴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Orchard(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Glader(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1451년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 차그퍼드에서 Agnes Crouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1480년 8월 30일 영국 데폰 차그퍼드에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Frederick Orchard nasceu em 1432, em Combe Martin, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Orchard, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Glader, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Crouche em 1451, em Chagford, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de agosto de 1480, em Chagford, Devon, Inglaterra, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Frederick Orchard родился в 1432 г. в местности комб мартинь, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Orchard было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Glader, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Crouche в 1451 г. в местности чагфорд, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 30 августа 1480 г. в местности чагфорд, девон, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Frederick Orchard nació en 1432, en Combe Martin, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Orchard, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Glader, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Agnes Crouche en 1451, en Chagford, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 30 de agosto de 1480, en Chagford, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años.
